today.title: Project
today.synopsis: Solo working block organised by Mike — no external attendees. Likely a dedicated focus or planning session.
today.prep_notes: Use this block to action in-product reviews: GDCU-8736 (Learning Hub imagery, Figma prototype awaiting your review) and GDCU-8801 (CA/FR oplist redesign). Also a good slot to follow up on the ACE AI daily reports issue flagged by Shane McGrath.

today.title: Prism CMS - Demo
today.synopsis: Gabriel Cornoiu demos his new Prism CMS — a headless CMS built to feed AI-generated apps, tools, and GDC pages. He wants the group to see it in action and share feedback.
discussion_topics: Prism CMS capabilities and architecture overview
Potential use cases: AI-generated content, tools, and GDC page feeds
Relationship to GDCU-8807 (Dynamic Content API with Cloudflare Workers + D1) — recently completed by Kelvin Yee
Fit within the current GDC roadmap and upcoming sprint backlog
today.prep_notes: Gabriel flagged in #gdc-pdmt (Apr 13) that he wants to refine Prism CMS further and discussed organising a dev session with Kelvin and Carlos. GDCU-8807 (Dynamic Content API) was completed this sprint — consider whether Prism CMS extends or overlaps that work. Come prepared with questions on roadmap fit, particularly for backlog tickets GDCU-8789 (ACE AI Embedded Betslips) and GDCU-8852 (Ace Assistant Widget). No Confluence agenda page found.

today.title: GDCU Assemble 💥 Standup
today.synopsis: Daily GDCU pod standup. First standup of Sprint 8Q2 — 41 tickets in scope, 13 in progress. Good opportunity to surface blockers and confirm hotfix priority for GDCU-8875.
discussion_topics: Sprint 8Q2 kick-off: 41 tickets, 13 in progress, 2 in QA, 2 in product
GDCU-8875 (hotfix: phone number on email landing page) — Chloe flagged as today's priority in #gdc-pod
GDCU-8548 🔥 (Sign-in trigger for Cosmo Spins) — flagged/blocked, assigned to Novica Vukobratovic
GDCU-8406 (hreflang fix for Slots pages) — reverted from In QA to In Progress by Sanja Budic
GDCU-8814 (XML Sitemaps missing 6,000+ URLs) — just pulled into sprint by Harden, high priority
ACE AI daily reports not generating since Saturday — Shane tagged Mike and Carlos in #gdc-pod
Sign-in bug on articles after sign out/sign in — Shane reported Apr 16 evening
today.prep_notes: Sprint 7 closed with 141 SP completed (Chloe announced in #gdc-pod Apr 16). Ciara has 7 carry-over tickets — agreed with Chloe to push slot tickets to next sprint. GDCU-8871 (Free to Play App POC v2) was moved back to To Do by Carlos — may need context. GDCU-8829 and GDCU-8808 were cancelled yesterday. Related Jira tickets: GDCU-8736, GDCU-8801 both in product awaiting your review.

today.title: Mike / Colin 1.1
today.synopsis: Weekly 1:1 with SVP Colin Brannigan. Colin has been closely watching the design system direction following Bruno's AI design system demo, and has asked pointed questions about prioritisation.
discussion_topics: Design system direction — Bruno's AI design system vs Ciara's Forge build; Mike's vision for component library ownership reducing design bottleneck
Sprint 8Q2 start and velocity: 141 SP delivered in Sprint 7, 41 tickets in Sprint 8Q2
Iceland launch: smoke testing passed, fixes still In Progress (GDCU-8856)
Cosmo Spins Spring Ad Campaign (GDCU-8846): Josh Kerr requested a rethink — less 'gaming feel', clearer message for users unfamiliar with Cosmo
Operator list redesign (GDCU-8619): currently blocked; CA/FR oplist (GDCU-8801): In Product
today.prep_notes: In your DM with Colin (Apr 16), he asked whether to pause everything and focus solely on the design system — you explained the vision clearly (component library ownership model, specialist projects still owned by design). Come prepared to reinforce this. Colin is watching it closely. Also be ready to give a brief update on Iceland post-launch status and Cosmo Spins campaign. No Confluence agenda found for this meeting.
